evals.prompt: I'm building a Qwik app and I need a counter component. It should have an increment and a decrement button, and display the current count. Show me how to do this correctly using Qwik's reactivity primitives.
evals.expected_output: A correct Qwik component using useSignal for the count, with onClick$ handlers on both buttons, demonstrating Qwik's fine-grained reactivity.

assertions.name: uses-useSignal
assertions.description: Response uses useSignal() to create reactive state

assertions.name: uses-onClick-dollar
assertions.description: Event handlers use the onClick$ QRL syntax

assertions.name: accesses-dot-value
assertions.description: Signal value is read/written via .value property

assertions.name: has-decrement
assertions.value: Does the response show a decrement button or handler that decrements the counter?

evals.prompt: In my Qwik City app I have a product detail page at src/routes/product/[id]/index.tsx. I want to fetch the product from my database on the server before rendering, so it's available in SSR. How do I do that, and how do I handle the case where the product isn't found?
evals.expected_output: Shows routeLoader$ with params.id, async fetch/db call, fail() for 404, and how to consume the loader in the component with signal.value.

assertions.name: uses-routeLoader-dollar
assertions.description: Response uses routeLoader$ for server-side data fetching

assertions.name: accesses-params-id
assertions.description: Response shows how to access the dynamic [id] param
assertions.value: Does the response show accessing params.id inside the loader?

assertions.name: handles-not-found
assertions.value: Does the response show how to handle the case when a product is not found?

assertions.name: consumes-in-component
assertions.description: Response shows consuming the loader result in the component
assertions.value: Does the response show calling the loader hook inside a component and using the signal value?

assertions.name: server-side-note
assertions.description: Response notes that routeLoader$ runs on the server
assertions.value: Does the response explain or mention that routeLoader$ runs on the server?

evals.prompt: I'm migrating from React and I don't understand why my code is breaking. I have a useEffect that runs only on the client to set up a third-party chart library, but in Qwik it seems like things work differently. Can you explain the Qwik lifecycle and show me the right way to initialize a non-serializable third-party library?
evals.expected_output: Explanation of resumability vs hydration, the useTask$/useVisibleTask$ lifecycle, and a concrete example of noSerialize() + useVisibleTask$ for the chart lib.

assertions.name: explains-resumability
assertions.description: Response explains resumability or the key difference from React hydration
assertions.value: Does the response explain Qwik's resumability model or the fundamental difference from React hydration?

assertions.name: recommends-useVisibleTask
assertions.description: Response recommends useVisibleTask$ for client-only initialization

assertions.name: uses-noSerialize
assertions.description: Response mentions noSerialize() for non-serializable values

assertions.name: shows-lifecycle-stages
assertions.description: Response shows or explains the three lifecycle stages
assertions.value: Does the response describe the Qwik lifecycle with useTask$, render, and useVisibleTask$ stages?

assertions.name: provides-code-example
assertions.description: Response includes a concrete code example for the third-party lib case
assertions.value: Does the response include a Qwik code example showing how to initialize a non-serializable third-party library?

assertions.name: maps-useEffect-to-useVisibleTask
assertions.description: Response maps React useEffect to Qwik useVisibleTask$
assertions.value: Does the response explicitly say that useVisibleTask$ is the Qwik equivalent of React's useEffect?
